message:
feat(brenner-loop): add confidence calibration tracking (x3xg)

Track user prediction accuracy over time to improve probability estimation:
- PredictionRecord and CalibrationMetrics types
- Brier score and Expected Calibration Error (ECE) calculation
- Over/under-confidence bias detection
- Domain-specific calibration analysis
- Resolution feedback with personalized advice
- Progress tracking over time
- 43 comprehensive tests

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-17wm","title":"Fresh-eyes audit: artifact-merge.ts deterministic merge logic","description":"# Goal
Fresh-eyes audit of the artifact merge logic (apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.ts), which is the core deterministic delta compilation engine.

This file is ~2,800 lines of critical protocol logic that:
- Parses structured deltas from agent messages
- Merges ADD/EDIT/KILL operations deterministically
- Validates artifact integrity (references, lifecycles)
- Produces canonical markdown output

## Scope
- Identify logic bugs, edge cases, or error handling gaps
- Check for potential race conditions or order-dependent bugs
- Review validation logic completeness
- Verify tests cover critical paths
- Add focused tests for any gaps found
- Fix obvious bugs (no deletions per AGENTS.md)

## Complements brenner_bot-ukzy
GrayBear is doing a broader audit across CLI, Agent Mail client, API routes, etc. This is a focused deep-dive on one critical file.

## Acceptance
- All identified bugs documented or fixed
- Test coverage verified/improved for artifact merge logic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonValley","created_at":"2026-01-01T20:46:18.588046977-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:49:59.533221275-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T20:49:59.533221275-05:00","close_reason":"Audit complete. No critical bugs found. Code is well-structured with proper security (prototype pollution protection), determinism (timestamp sorting, deep cloning), idempotency (KILL ops), and comprehensive test coverage (108 tests passing). Shared findings with GrayBear and OrangePuma."}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","title":"Tutorial UX: Mobile Experience & Limitations","description":"# Purpose

Document and handle the reality that tutorial steps requiring terminal commands cannot be fully completed on mobile devices. Ensure mobile users have a good experience anyway.

---

# The Reality

Many tutorial steps require:
- Running terminal commands (git, bun, ./brenner)
- Copying multi-line code blocks
- Running AI agents (Claude Code, Codex)

**These cannot be done on mobile.** A phone or tablet user physically cannot:
- Run a terminal
- Clone a repo
- Execute CLI commands

---

# Design Decisions

## Option A: Gate terminal steps to desktop (CHOSEN)

Show a clear message on terminal-required steps when on mobile:
\`\`\`
📱 This step requires a desktop computer

The commands below need to be run in a terminal on your 
Mac, Windows (WSL), or Linux computer.

You can:
• Read through this step to understand what's involved
• Bookmark this page and return on desktop
• Continue to the next step (some content is mobile-friendly)
\`\`\`

## Option B: Mobile-optimized read-only mode

Automatically switch to "read-only" mode on mobile:
- All content visible
- Code blocks show but note "run on desktop"
- Progress tracking still works
- Can complete "understanding" checkboxes, not "execution" checkboxes

---

# Implementation

## 1. Device Detection
Use user-agent or viewport width to detect mobile.

## 2. Step Metadata
Add \`requiresDesktop: boolean\` to step data:
\`\`\`typescript
interface TutorialStep {
  // ...existing fields
  requiresDesktop?: boolean;
  mobileAlternative?: string; // What to show on mobile instead
}
\`\`\`

## 3. Mobile Message Component
\`\`\`tsx
function MobileDesktopGate({ children, step }: Props) {
  const isMobile = useIsMobile();
  
  if (isMobile && step.requiresDesktop) {
    return <MobileNotice step={step} />;
  }
  
  return children;
}
\`\`\`

## 4. Which Steps Need Desktop?

**Quick Start**:
- Step 2 (Prerequisites): Desktop required
- Step 3 (Clone & Install): Desktop required
- Step 4 (Search Corpus): Desktop required
- Step 5 (Build Excerpt): Desktop required
- Step 6 (First Session): Desktop required
- Step 7 (Understand Output): Mobile OK (reading)

**Agent-Assisted**:
- Steps 2-7: Desktop required
- Step 1, 8: Mobile OK (reading/understanding)

**Multi-Agent**:
- All steps: Desktop required (infrastructure setup)

---

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mobile detection works reliably
- [ ] Desktop-required steps show clear message on mobile
- [ ] Users can still read step content on mobile
- [ ] Progress tracking works on mobile (marks steps as "viewed")
- [ ] Desktop users see no change","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:34:28.987529053-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:08:48.244621638-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:35:25.632137409-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

- [ ] Desktop users see no change","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftRiver","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:34:28.987529053-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-05T20:58:31.73574274-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:35:25.632137409-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5","title":"Spec: Experiment Result Classification and Interpretation","description":"# Goal
Define how experiment results are classified and interpreted into DELTAs.

# Two-Stage Process (Key Insight)

**Stage 1: Classification** (structured, can be assisted)
- What KIND of result is this?
- Pass/fail/error/metrics/observation/anomaly?

**Stage 2: Interpretation** (judgment, guidelines only)
- What does this mean for hypotheses?
- What DELTA should be created?

# Stage 1: Result Classification

## The Taxonomy (Restored)
```typescript
type ExperimentResultType = 
  | "pass"           // Test completed, outcome matches expectation
  | "fail"           // Test completed, outcome contradicts expectation
  | "error"          // Test couldn't complete (crash, timeout, missing deps)
  | "metrics"        // Quantitative measurements without clear pass/fail
  | "observation"    // Qualitative finding requiring interpretation
  | "anomaly";       // Unexpected result that doesn't fit any prediction

interface ClassifiedResult {
  experiment_id: string;
  test_id: string;
  
  // Classification
  result_type: ExperimentResultType;
  confidence: "high" | "medium" | "low";
  
  // Extracted data (type-specific)
  metrics?: Record<string, number>;
  observation?: string;
  error_message?: string;
  
  // Which hypotheses this relates to
  hypothesis_refs: string[];  // ["H1", "H2"]
}
```

## Classification CLI (Assistant, Not Automator)
```bash
# Suggest classification based on output
./brenner.ts experiment classify \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3

# Output:
# Result type: metrics (confidence: high)
# Extracted metrics:
#   bleu_vanilla: 0.42
#   bleu_rrp: 0.48
#   bleu_freq_penalty: 0.45
# 
# Related hypotheses: H1, H2 (based on test T1 definition)
# 
# Suggested interpretation pattern: "metrics_comparison"
# See: ./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern metrics_comparison
```

# Stage 2: Interpretation Patterns

Classification tells you WHAT you have. Interpretation decides WHAT IT MEANS.

## Pattern: pass → Predictions Update
**When**: Test passed, result matches hypothesis prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "predictions_table",
  "target_id": "P1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_H1": "Confirmed: 0.03 BLEU difference, within equivalence threshold"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 passed: equivalence holds at tested scale"
}
```

## Pattern: fail → Hypothesis Weakening
**When**: Test clearly contradicts a hypothesis
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "hypothesis_slate",
  "target_id": "H1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "weakened",
    "evidence_against": "T1 showed 15% quality gap at 16k context"
  },
  "rationale": "H1 predicted equivalence; T1 found significant divergence"
}
```

## Pattern: error → Test Blocked
**When**: Experiment couldn't complete
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "blocker": "OOM at 16k context, need 32GB instance"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 could not complete due to resource constraints"
}
```

## Pattern: metrics → Attach Raw Data
**When**: Have numbers but interpretation unclear
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_results": {
      "bleu_vanilla": 0.42,
      "bleu_rrp": 0.48,
      "bleu_freq_penalty": 0.45
    },
    "status": "awaiting_interpretation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 produced metrics. Interpretation TBD."
}
```

## Pattern: anomaly → Register for Investigation
**When**: Result doesn't match ANY prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "anomaly_register",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Latency spike at 4k boundary",
    "observation": "RRP showed 3x latency at exactly 4096 tokens",
    "conflicts_with": ["H1", "H2"],
    "status": "active"
  },
  "rationale": "No hypothesis predicted latency effects"
}
```

## Pattern: observation → Critique or Assumption
**When**: Qualitative insight from running experiment
**DELTA** (if it challenges framing):
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "adversarial_critique",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Metric choice confound",
    "attack": "BLEU may not capture the coherence benefits RRP provides",
    "evidence": "Running T1 revealed outputs differed in structure, not token overlap",
    "current_status": "Observed during experiment, needs investigation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 execution revealed potential metric confound"
}
```

# When Patterns Don't Fit
- If result is ambiguous between patterns → discuss in thread
- If multiple patterns apply → create multiple DELTAs
- If no pattern fits → add to anomaly_register with status: "unclassified"

# CLI Helper
```bash
# Show interpretation guidance for a pattern
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern anomaly

# Interactive mode: walk through classification and interpretation
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --interactive \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ExperimentResultType taxonomy defined
- [ ] ClassifiedResult schema defined
- [ ] experiment classify command implemented
- [ ] All interpretation patterns documented in specs/
- [ ] experiment interpret helper command
- [ ] Guidance on when to ask for help

# Relationship to Other Work
- Depends on: Execution protocol (brenner_bot-t62l)
- Uses: Existing delta-parser and artifact-merge
- Enables: Artifact evolution from experiment evidence","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:51:12.453215707-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 child tasks completed: ExperimentResult schema (1es5.1), artifact types (1es5.2), experiment encode CLI (1es5.3), golden fixtures + tests (1es5.4)","labels":["infrastructure","protocol","research-loop"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-t62l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:52:25.940633427-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:59:54.950352935-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5.1","title":"Task: Finalize ExperimentResult schema + delta mapping rules","description":"# Goal
Lock a strict, testable contract for how `ExperimentResult` attaches to the canonical artifact.

This is **encoding**, not interpretation.

---

# Inputs
- `ExperimentResult` JSON (from `experiment run` or `experiment record`)
- Operator-provided linkage:
  - which thread
  - which test (`T#`)

---

# Outputs
- One or more DELTA blocks that update the artifact deterministically.

---

# Decisions to lock
## Where do run records live in the artifact?
Preferred: extend `discriminative_tests` items with minimal optional fields, e.g.:
- `last_run_at`
- `last_exit_code`
- `last_result_path` (relative path)
- `last_summary` (short)

Avoid:
- embedding full stdout/stderr in the artifact
- inventing a sprawling “lab notebook” section

## Mapping from `test_id` to artifact item
- Option A: require `test_id` matches the artifact test `name` prefix (e.g., `T1: ...`)
- Option B: store `test_id` as an explicit field on tests going forward

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Representation documented with examples
- [ ] Clear mapping from result JSON → artifact fields
- [ ] Provenance rule: always include command + cwd + timestamps + result path
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:03:06.113946274-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:35:51.822267438-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:35:51.822267438-05:00","close_reason":"Created specs/experiment_result_encoding_v0.1.md with extended DiscriminativeTest schema, mapping rules, examples for success/failure/timeout, and error cases","labels":["experiments","protocol","results"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:03:06.115040905-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5.2","title":"Task: Extend artifact types/rendering for experiment result fields","description":"# Goal
Ensure whatever result→DELTA encoding we adopt is actually representable and renderable in the canonical artifact.

---

# Background
Current artifact schema v0.1 has 7 sections and no explicit "results" section.
If we add new fields (e.g., `actual_results` on tests), we must update:
- Type definitions (apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.ts)
- Merge behavior (applyEdit / applyAdd)
- Canonical markdown renderer (renderArtifactMarkdown)
- Linter rules if needed

---

# Non-goal
Do not create a sprawling "lab notebook" section. We want minimal, decision-relevant results.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Chosen representation is supported end-to-end (merge + render + lint)
- [ ] No silent dropping of result fields
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:03:19.93887011-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:41:06.981520151-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:41:06.981520151-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in 176c853: Added TestLastRun, TestStatus types; extended TestItem with experiment fields; updated renderTests() for result display","labels":["artifact-schema","compiler","experiments"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:03:19.969010924-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:37:11.632809309-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

@@ -549,6 +549,57 @@ export {
   getResultColor,
 } from "./evidence";
 
+// ============================================================================
+// Calibration Tracking (bead x3xg)
+// ============================================================================
+
+export type {
+  PredictionRecord,
+  CalibrationBin,
+  CalibrationMetrics,
+  ResolutionFeedback,
+  CalibrationProgress,
+} from "./calibration";
+
+export {
+  // Constants
+  DEFAULT_BIN_BOUNDARIES,
+  CALIBRATION_ERROR_THRESHOLD,
+  MIN_PREDICTIONS_FOR_METRICS,
+  MIN_PREDICTIONS_PER_BIN,
+
+  // Record creation
+  generatePredictionId,
+  createPredictionRecord,
+  resolvePrediction,
+
+  // Calibration calculation
+  binPredictions,
+  calculateBrierScore,
+  calculateCalibrationError,
+  calculateBias,
+  calculateSharpness,
+  calculateDomainAccuracy,
+  calculateCalibrationMetrics,
+
+  // Feedback generation
+  getCalibrationAtConfidence,
+  generateResolutionFeedback,
+
+  // Progress tracking
+  trackCalibrationProgress,
+
+  // Utility functions
+  formatBrierScore,
+  formatCalibrationError,
+  getCalibrationQualityAssessment,
+  identifyCalibrationStrengths,
+
+  // Type guards
+  isPredictionOutcome,
+  isPredictionRecord,
+} from "./calibration";
+
